content.problemStatement:
Chef found an array AAA of NNN elements. He defines a subarray as bad if the maximum element of the subarray is equal to the minimum element of the subarray.

More formally, a subarray [L,R][L,R][L,R] (1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N)(1 \le L \le R \le N)(1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N) is bad if max(AL,AL+1,‚Ä¶,AR)=min(AL,AL+1,...,AR)\texttt{max}(A_L, A_{L+1}, \ldots , A_R) = \texttt{min} (A_L, A_{L+1}, ... , A_R)max(AL‚Äã,AL+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AR‚Äã)=min(AL‚Äã,AL+1‚Äã,...,AR‚Äã).

Chef is allowed to change **at most** KKK elements of the array.

Find the **minimum** number of bad subarrays Chef can obtain after changing **at most** KKK elements of the array.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input, the first line contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK - the number of elements in the array and the maximum number of elements Chef can change respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2,\ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã - the initial array.


Output Format
For each test case, output the **minimum** number of bad subarrays Chef can obtain after changing **at most** KKK elements of the array.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3‚ãÖ1053\cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The minimum number of bad subarrays Chef can obtain is 333. Chef does not need to perform any operations. The subarrays where the maximum element is equal to the minimum element are [1,1],[2,2],[1,1], [2,2],[1,1],[2,2], and [3,3][3,3][3,3] where [L,R][L,R][L,R] denotes the subarray starting at index LLL and ending at index RRR.
Test case 222: The minimum number of bad subarrays Chef can obtain is 444. Chef can perform 222 operations and change the array to [2,4,3,1][2, 4, 3, 1][2,4,3,1]. The subarrays where the maximum element is equal to the minimum element are [1,1],[2,2],[3,3],[1,1], [2,2], [3,3],[1,1],[2,2],[3,3], and [4,4][4,4][4,4].
